entry.title: Good Afternoon all,
entry.content:
I am new to this forum, but looking for some assistance!

I am developing the 856 Doc DESADV output but having trouble with all the necessary date flowing through to my output document, I am missing all the customer uom, part number, qty ordered, etc.

This information is setup in our system but I do not know how to get it on the delivery. 

Pretty sure there is some copy controls needed but no clue where to start. 

Any help would be greatly appreciated!


Thank you,

Tamra.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition | 2023 FPS02 Release – Part 1
entry.content:
Great news!

when should i start looking for the template on CAL?

2022 edition was there in July 2023.

thx,

gm

entry.title: Re: Restoring Sap Hana cloud instance using a database ba...
entry.content:
The equivalent SAP HANA Cloud documentation is here: https://help.sap.com/docs/hana-cloud-database/sap-hana-cloud-sap-hana-database-deployment-infrastructure-hdi-reference/import-sap-hdi-container-for-copy-purposes

That should provide you with the necessary information to import the HDI container that you had previously exported. 

 

Note that exporting an HDI container can be used as a method to backup an individual HDI container, but that is quite different from creating a full database backup. The good news is that SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA database supports importing HDI containers.

entry.title: Difference in Configuration between SAP ALE and SAP EDI
entry.content: What is the difference between Configuration of ALE and EDI in SAP?

entry.title: Re: Crystal Report Viewer Chart Font Issue
entry.content:
R&D had another look at the chart and here's how to fix it:

Hi Don,

The difference of the rendering caused by GDI+ could be quite hard to fix…

However after I investigated more on this report, I found the issue could be workaround…just open report in CRDesigner, right click the chart and select “Chart Expert…”, then switch to the Text tab:

Select the Group title, then click Font button on leftside, you could see the Bold was selected as Font style, just change it to Regular then click OK.

Also change the Data title as well, click OK button on Chart Expert dialog to save the change. Save the report and view it in Winform Viewer:

It looks same as in CRDesigner now😊

entry.title: Re: failed to connect to SAP B1 HANA server.
entry.content:
Hi @Andre_S,

I had the same error with the installer and I found out that on other Windows accounts I didn't had the same problem, so I deleted the Windows account and it's files from C:\Users and recreated it, or just use other account.

entry.title: Re: Unable to access SAP Build Code Trial Account
entry.content:
Hi @Satinder1 

I experienced this issue and fixed it by assigning the role collections Build Code Developer and Build Code Administrator to my custom IAS user (not default identity provider).

entry.title: Re: SAP Companion content - Moving from test to production
entry.content:
Hi Marie,

There could be a number of issues:

1. The SAP Companion configuration is not correct or complete in Production
2. If you are trying to access the content using and Enable Now Learner account, the content must be published in the Production WA
3. The Workarea Permissions may not have been setup to allow users to access content from the Production WA

My initial guess is it's the first item that is the problem but it's impossible to say without more information.  You can either post a screenshot of your SAP Companion configuration here so I can check or raise a support ticket using the Component ID KM-SEN-CMP and get someone to take a closer look.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: Why Failure-Mode Focus Matters in Intelligent Asset Management
entry.content:
Very interesting post, Elmar!
I would like to hear your thoughts on the use of Classes in an E2E maintenance scenario: APM functionality kind of assumes that Classes are defined and used, a.o. through the "Manage Strategy Assessments for Classes" app, where it is indicated (but not implemented?) that the same principles will apply for a.o. FMEA. But there is nothing really forcing anyone to use Classes, is there? Object types will often seem to serve the same purpose, but ... will it really? 

Also, as reliability engineering will often have either a function-oriented point of view or at least a system-oriented point of view, could you say something about your thinking along the lines of system objects - in APM and S/4 both? Clearly, assessments are often made on the basis that you want to assure and assess issues for a certain system or functional performance, meaning that you need to look at a somewhat bigger picture and not "just" a technical object. 

Much appreciate your thoughts on this!

entry.title: Re: Upgrade SAP enabale Now 2406 start ManagerSEN failled
entry.content:
Hi,

Unfortunately, it's difficult to say why you have an issue so you will need to raise an SAP support ticket using the Component ID KM-SEN-MGR and get someone to have a look. 

Regards
Shane

My actua XML file:

The requested structure for the bank:

I need to brake each invoice detail for one tag <Strd>     </Strd>

 

Could you help me please with some idea?

 

Tks in advance

 

Elver

entry.title: Show more than one currency in the same planning view
entry.content:
Hello experts,

I want to display more than one currency in the same planning view

For example, I want to see the price of a product in Dollar and Euro, both of them at the same time in the same planning view. How can I do that?

Thanks a lot for your help,

Alicia

entry.title: Re: Expose Fiori Launchpad to public internet only specif...
entry.content: You need to talk to your firewall team if they have a Web Application Firewall. This could be used to restrict access to users that belong to a specific Group in your identity provider.

entry.title: Re: How to generate a SAML assertion by Using SAP Cloud Identity Services (IAS)
entry.content: Can you provide details which target system you want to connect via the live data connection?

entry.title: Re: BLOQUEO ARTICULOS COMPROMETIDOS
entry.content: Que tal Agustin, tienes algún manual para esto que comentaste?

entry.title: Re: Enable Now - PowerBI Data Point for User Roles?
entry.content:
Hi Andrew,

I wish there was an easy answer to this question but I do not know it.  What I know is:

1. If you download and run the Administrative Insights report/dashboard that is available from this page in Power BI you will get a tonne of information about your entire environment
2. Included in the information that is generated using this report is a table called Effective User and this table contains user details and the Roles assigned to users
3. You can export this information into an Excel spreadsheet

So, the information is not a list of Roles and users with those Roles but it does at least allow you to see the Roles assigned to every user.

Outside that, I would raise a support ticket using the Component ID KM-SEN-MGR and get advice about how to generate the list.  I'm pretty certain that you will need to do this using a third-party reporting tool, like Power BI, because there is no standard report but I suspect that you will not have been the first person to ask for this type of report.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Design iFlow to accept multiple PDF Files using the HTTP Send Adapter
entry.content:
Hi folks

I have a requirement to call a 3rd party REST API which requires 2 separate PDF files as input parameters. Can someone kindly explain how I can design an iFLow in CPI that can accept two PDF files as input using the HTTP Sender Adapter. 

An alternative solution to the HTTP Sender Adapter is also acceptable. 

Thank You.

Vijhay D.

entry.title: Integrating Onboarding and Learning For New Hires Before Day-1
entry.content:
SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding solution allows a new hire to access information about the organization and its culture before his/her start date. It also provides access to tools and information that the new hire requires to become a productive member of the team before the start date. There are several key benefits of integrating your new hire onboarding program with a Learning Management System (LMS). The Learning integration allows you to enhance your onboarding/orientation material with courses that the new hires will need such as regulations, organizational policies, IT guides, and regional laws to name a few.

Once you've set up this integration, data from Onboarding is exported to Learning in the time period of your choice. This allows learning admins to assign courses for the new hires.

The new hires can log in to the home page before the start date and access the Take Courses to-do tile. New hires can also navigate to Learning from home page menu drop-down.

1- Extract Onboardee Data Using Integration Center:

Building integration for Onboarding will be easier and quicker if you have already switched to extract data from Employee Central using Integration Center instead of using the Employees Export scheduled job in Provisioning.
Creating the Onboarding new hire’s User Data feed involves creating and scheduling the Integration Center job and modifying the SF USER CONNECTOR properties file in the LMS system configuration.

  • Create a simple output integration file to export Onboarding New hire user data
  • Select starting entity = ONB2Process
  • Configure fields

Note: To set up the integration you need to have minimum fields in your export file.

  1. STATUS (Can be extracted through the path userNav/status)
  2. USERID
  3. FIRSTNAME (Can be extracted through the path userNav/firstName)
  4. LASTNAME (Can be extracted through the path userNav/lastName)
  5. PERSON_GUID (Can be extracted through the path userNav/personKeyNav/perPersonUuid)
  6. PERSON_ID_EXTERNAL (Can be extracted through the path userNav/personKeyNav/personIdExternal)
  7. PROCESS ID
  8. PROCESS STATUS
  • Add filters
  • Set up Destination setting
  • Schedule Integration job to export data on SFTP for LMS consumption

 

Note: You need to make sure that you select the header type in your integration to avoid any error with LMS integration.

 

Also, design your integration export file header using Configure Fields>Advanced Header View

Please refer Header is missing in the output file - SuccessFactors Integrations KBA for more details.

 2- Update Learning System Configuration for Integration:

You need to modify the connector properties file to add the Onboarding external user status. This file is then used to sync the Onboarding data to Learning.

  • Navigate to Admin Center>Learning Administrator>System Administration>Configuration>System Configuration>CONNECTORS
  • In the system configuration:1- Search for the Configuration Key “sfuser.field.transform.data.NOTACTIVE.N” and add          active_external_suite.
    2- Search for the Configuration Key “sfuser.field.transform.data.NOTACTIVE.N” and add inactive_external_suite.
    3- Search for the Configuration Key “sfuser.field.transform.data.SHOPPING_ACCT_TYPE.INTERNAL” and add active_external_suite,inactive_external_suite.
    4- Search for the Configuration Key “sfuser.connector.input.file.multiple.format” and make sure you have this configuration available TIMESTAMP_BEFORE_EXTENSION.
    5- If it isn't already available, add sfuser.connector.input.file.header.skip.records.count=0 setting in the Connector file.
  • Select Apply Changes.
  • Set allowFutureHireDates=true in the “System Configuration” file. By default, this “allowFutureHireDates” will be set as false under Configuration> System Configuration>LMS_ADMIN 

3- Create Assignment Profiles for Onboarding New hires in LMS:

Make sure LMS administrators have set up learning assignments for Onboarding new hires based on business requirements.  Please refer to the following link for more information.

SAP SuccessFactors Learning Assignment Profiles

Assignment Profiles Best Practices in SuccessFactors Learning

4- Schedule LMS job:

Schedule a Learning job to ensure that learning assignments are set up for onboarding new hires according to your business criteria.

 

 
5- Set up Role-based permissions:
Onboarding External User role must be granted the Learning permission to access the Learning module.
 

6- Onboardee Experience:

The new hires can log in to the home page before the start date and access the Take Courses to-do tile. New hires can also navigate to Learning from home page menu drop-down.

Conclusion:

Integrating onboarding and learning for new hires before their first day not only accelerates their acclimation but also empowers them with the tools and knowledge they need to hit the ground running. By delivering personalized learning experiences early on, organizations foster a sense of belonging and competence from day one. This proactive approach improves engagement, reduces time to productivity, and enhances retention, ultimately contributing to a more seamless and efficient onboarding process. As businesses continue to prioritize employee experience, pre-day 1 learning integration stands out as a strategic advantage in shaping a future-ready workforce.

entry.title: Re: Set Default Quotation Valid to Date
entry.content: Thanks for the explaination, you've sent me down the right path. However, while trying it, the date calculation did not work until I went into the "Assign Custom Routines to Sales Document Types" activity to assign the newly created routine to the Quotation document type. Is this additional step mandatory?

entry.title: Re: IDOC DELVRY07, Segment E1ADRM1, Field STREET2 is not filled
entry.content: Hi my friend, check snote: https://me.sap.com/notes/357943/E

entry.title: Re: Dynamic Agent (Parallel) Keeping Approver on Hold
entry.content:
There are solutions which could be built where you send the request back to the Initiator and ask for correction. The part where the approval is only sought is tricky as that means we read the workflow log and identify based on that.

I would say why don't you just build a rejection path for a particular loop. So when user rejects, it simply creates a loop within that path without triggering the merge badi. That way you can simply reroute and proceed.

I didn’t see Custom groups at data model level to define. Any screenshots or reference blogs for these features.

Regards, Vasu

entry.title: How can I define a second consecutive non-work day
entry.content:
Hi Expert,

I have a question on how to retrieve a previous non-workday using the Time Record Filter tool.
My requirement is to calculate OT1.5 when an employee works on a none scheduled workday and OT2.0 when the employee works on a second consecutive none scheduled workday.

If it cannot be done via the Time Filter Record, please share other solution to this requirement.
Thank you!
SAP SuccessFactors Time Tracking HCM Time Management SAP Timesheet

entry.title: Re: How to convert columns into rows in Webi report
entry.content: You need to create new variables Category, YTD and PFY with corresponding if-statements to separate the information and then you can insert them into a table independently

entry.title: SAP Stammtisch München (Nov 2024)
entry.content:
Liebe Stammtischschwestern und -brüder,

wir treffen uns am Donnerstag, den 14.11., zum SAP Stammtisch.
Lasst uns in schönem gemütlichen Ambiente zum Thema SAP und drumherum austauschen.

Wir treffen uns tbd. Bitte haltet auch eure Zu- und Absage aktuell, damit wir die richtige Personenzahl für unsere Planung haben.

Wir freuen uns auf Euer kommen und einen regen Austausch!
SAP Stammtisch Orga Team

entry.title: Re: Calculation View Currency conversion with decimal shi...
entry.content: did you try to let HANA create the target with "Generate result currency column" ?

entry.title: MDG Custom entity refresh on change, Empty custom entity
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I would like to pass on my experience from a recent upgrading event; I'm documenting it here so that anyone who encounters this particular issue will have a reference and a solution to address it.


In MDG, building a custom entity is a very simple and well-documented process; unfortunately, after our system upgrade, custom entities ceased showing information or maintaining data on changes in NWBC. 

I recreated tones of custom entities and subsequent UIBBs on screen, but their behavior was similar across all custom entities. I checked every component of the GenIL or MDG models, as well as every custom enhancement or handler class.
After spending hours and hours trying to figure out the problem, we discovered that SAP had recently made corrections for performance optimization, one of which was an authorization check on entities. 

The change was applied to standard handler classes, specifically in method "IF_MDG_BS_BP_ACCESS_HANDLER~CHECK_AUTHORITY_BY_ENTITY". This method is now obsolete and all logic is moved under "IF_MDG_BS_BP_ACCESS_HANDLER~CHECK_AUTHORITY_MASS_BY_ENTITY". However, the fix included a warning message in the obsolete method, which caused authority checks to fail, causing data refresh issues on custom entities.

This fix is in SAP Note 3406917.
I hope this helps someone save a lot of time on debugging.

entry.title: Re: SAP BAS - CDS Watch does not display data
entry.content: I produced some csv files. The structure of the project is basically standard.

entry.title: Annualized compensation planning: keeping EC aligned with Compensation Statements
entry.content:
This blog was made as a reply to this question on another blog.

 

Introduction

When employees in an SAP SuccessFactors environment have Pay Components in different frequencies (hourly, monthly, semi-monthly, annual) on their profile yet annual Compensation planning is made based on annualized values (see Option 2 here) slight annual salary value discrepancies (by a decimal point most of the time) between EC and Compensation statements can happen which can impact the consistency of communication to employees.

 

Example

  • Pay Component value in EC = 3,500
  • Units per year = 26 (frequency of the Pay Component)
  • Pay Component Group value (curSalary in Compensation template) = 91,000  (3,500*26)
  • Merit amount entered by planner in the Compensation worksheet = 9,000 / merit % is hence equal to ((100,000-91,000)/91,000)*100=9.8901 which rounds to 9.89%
  • "Final Annualized Salary" standard column (finSalary) in the Comp worksheet now shows 100,000
  • To publish to EC we need to divide 100,000 by the frequency used in the Pay Component which gives us 100,000/26=3,846.153846153846 --> 3,846.15 after half-up rounding (assuming two decimals)
  • Once 3,846.15 is published back, the Pay Component Group in EC will (re)multiply it by 12 to get its value (and we cannot change this behavior) --> 3,846.15*26=99,999.9  the Annualized Salary in EC will show 99,999.9 whereas on the Compensation worksheets and on statements (which can only get their value from Compensation worksheets as of 2H 2024) will show 100,000.

 

Here is the list of ideas I've explored that either fix the issue temporarily (meaning it will happen again in the following cycle) / or bring data consistency risks for the comp cycle, EC data or payroll replication : Removing decimals from the money format all together, hiding the EC Pay Component Group from employees in Comp Info while statements are out, playing with publish back dates, playing with Pay Component decimals (max 5)....

 

Proposed configuration adjustment with limited impact on the planner experience

Here is what I built for a customer a few years ago which they've been using ever since :

  1. Hide the amount from the standard merit column and only allow planners to enter a % (a specific section with custom columns can be created to allow planners to find the right percentage based on an amount if needed)
  2. Apply the merit % entered by planners NOT to the Annualized Salary column (curSalary) BUT to the Pay Component value we brought from EC into a custom column using the configuration below (assuming that as per leading practices there is at least one Pay Component for each employee that has the setting : Use for Comp = Comp or Use for Comp = Both)

  1. We now have a first standard column Merit % (editable) then another read-only custom column Merit Amount where the formula is: customCurrentPayRate*(merit/curSalary)
  2. Finally add two columns :
    • Final Pay Rate: customCurrentPayRate+customMeritAmount (will be used for publish back)
    • Final Annualized Salary: customFinalPayRate*26

 

 

Another design idea with a higher impact on the planner experience (the sum of current pay + what they entered as recommendation is different by a few decimals from what gets published back to EC) is to keep the standard merit column behavior with both % and amount editable and to simply have two columns in the "Final" group of columns: 

  • Final Adjusted Pay Rate where decimals are removed (see this blog for more information on removing decimals using custom rounding). This value is also to be used for publish back.
  • Final Adjusted Annual Salary which multiplies the Final Adjusted Pay Rate by the Pay Component frequency.

 

 

All the best

Xavier

entry.title: Re: Dates and Roundings in Compensation
entry.content:
@MicheleCordano @Arpita_01 

Apologies for the delay, I was on parental leave when you posted your question and only catching up with this now.

I believe your EC design may be a bit different from our leading practices and you have a rounding issue that most customers don't have (but they do sometimes have another similar one which I will explain below). Can you share a screenshot of your Yearly and Monthly values in EC ?

The issue I know of only happens with the publish back design and only when planning is done based on annualized values while Pay Components are in various frequencies. I just documented it here: 

https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blogs-by-sap/annualized-compensation-planning-keeping-ec-aligned-with-compensation/ba-p/13894512#M7786

All the best

Xavier

entry.title: Re: Order type of the sales order created with solution o...
entry.content:
Hi Brad,
Thank you for your answer.
I understand how to create a ticket.

Soma

entry.title: Re: New Financial Accounting for Service Management in S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
SVO2, fixed price service orders, do not use confirmation based billing, so EBRR key CCOCF is not a suitable.  As per doco: This method is not suitable for fixed-price item categories. For fixed-price items, there is no unbilled revenue for single confirmations since the revenue is based on the net value on the service order item level. With fixed-price items, the item itself is billed, not the confirmations.

Which key can be used to calcuate unbilled revenue  at the service order item level in service management with the Event-Based Revenue Recognition job? What is the best practice for fixed price service orders using bundles?

entry.title: Re: New Financial Accounting for Service Management in S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Service order items are now accounting objects and can be posted to via journals, activity allocations and reassign cost and revenue app even after a service order item has been completed and finally billed. 

What status on the service order prevents any financial postings after it has been released? Service order items can still have financial journals posted to it even if completed or rejected.

entry.title: Re: Issue with Deep Entity Request in CAP (cds.validate issue - cds v 8.3.0)
entry.content: The bug is still there!

entry.title: Re: Offshore vs. Onshore Instance: China Subsidiary-based Rollout for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Editi
entry.content:
Hi Jochem,

From a technical standpoint, you can refer to the offshore sample where there is a central single instance in the US onshore, and in China and Germany offshore. However, to apply this use case, you or your customer will need to consult a local legal advisor about the relevant regulations. Additionally, you need to consider that not all the services included in the Grow with SAP offering in US/EU are available in China.

From a commercial perspective, I think this use case is likely applicable to Chinese customers who operate their business internationally.

Best regards,

Robert

entry.title: Document Generation WF template in a future dated Pay components change WF
entry.content:
Hey Guru's,

I have configured the Document generation WF templates for some of the WFs in the system. When I try to use a business rule to fetch Pay component amount/ frequency the output is blank if the effective date of the change is in the future. I have tried to change the field type to be string or number but no output is given if the change is in the future. Anyone has an idea how to fix this?

 

Output: Effective Date in the future

 

SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central

entry.title: Re: Migration Cockpit Error
entry.content:
Hi, please check out the following link, it should solve your problem:

3505612 - No local curr.type configured for company code (Migration Cockpit error) - SAP for Me

And when you faced error messages again, you can always try to search knowledge base on SAP for me

entry.title: Re: FTE and Proration examples in Compensation
entry.content:
Hi @xavierlegarrec, 

Thanks for sharing your COMP knowledge, it has been very useful. 🙂

With proration - does the functionality calculates the average FTE of employees during that fiscal year? For example, the employee is full time (i.e. 1 FTE) for half of the fiscal year, and then switched to 0.5 FTE for the second half of the fiscal year. How would the proration work in this scenario? Is the system smart enough to calculate the average FTE is 0.75? 

Thanks,
Kathy

entry.title: Re: Integration between S/4Hana Public Cloud and non-SAP...
entry.content:
Hi,

S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition can integrate with SAP or non-SAP systems.

The first step I would suggest is you can check the API list page All | SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition | SAP Business Accelerator Hub

Find out from business perspective, which business data you would like to integrate with S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Then you can always find out the communication scenario, and then create the communication arrangement based on that. From there, you can integrate with your external systems

entry.title: Re: Visualization for Mobile Services Destinations using SAP Mobile Backend Tools and OData CSDL Mod
entry.content:

what about if your app security is configured with IAS

and your mobile destination is configured with Forward Authentication, will it work?

thanks

entry.title: Re: FTE and Proration examples in Compensation
entry.content: Hi @catlin no not the Compensation templates however in Variable Pay templates you would be able to pick up the FTE changes throughout the year.

entry.title: Re: Combine CS15 and CS03
entry.content:
For CS03：Which explode the components from the header material.

For CS15： which check the component where be used, to find the  header material.

In another words, the CS03 and  CS15 are the different direction to display the BOM, so it cannot combine them.

Besides, for MMBE which display the stock of material, which saved in the table MARD, you can contact your developer to create a report, thank you!

entry.title: Re: Add BP Role UKM000 in Customer Migration Template

Customer - extend existing record by credit management data | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Re: exit variable with "E_TH_CHAS"
entry.content: The error : Missing attribute value in new row/column (Currency Field)

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Integration Suite Deploy Undeploy Config tool Part 2
entry.content: Hi @fatima4 - Thanks for clarifying. How much time it took to deploy or undeploy those 40 artifacts? Also what about the splitter point I raised? How about we use PartnerDirectory/Value map to store downtime period per business system and undeploy the relevant flows?

entry.title: Re: CIG transaction tracker handling
entry.content: Monitoring is made easy when CIG is integrated with Cloud ALM.

entry.title: Re: CIG transaction tracker handling
entry.content: Monitoring is made easy when CIG is integrated with SAP Cloud ALM for Operations • Notification of error messages can be automated • Notifications can be grouped based on document type • In CIG the retention period for transactions and their payloads is only 30 days where as in Cloud ALM the retention period can be set up to 12 months

entry.title: Re: How do I calculate the too new to evaluate, with othe...
entry.content:
Hi Fabi1,

If the "Too new to rate" is selected the actual rating will be treated as null (unrated).

2078689 - Default Rating And Unrated-Rating Values in Performance Management 

BR,
Yilin

entry.title: Re: 2H2024 - Delegation Available for Performance Reviews
entry.content:
Hi CMezger,

I'm afraid your desired feature behavior is not supported yet.
The form template and the delegation valid period need to be specified when creating the delegation.

Please refer to the Help Portal below for details on how the delegation works currently with performance review:
Delegation for Performance Reviews

BR,
Yilin

entry.title: SAC Hierarchy node open to left
entry.content:
Hi Everyone 

Is there any way that my hierarchy that is in columns open to the left??

Normal behavior:

 

Desired

SAP Analytics Cloud 

Thanks

entry.title: RAP - Sales Order Payment BO
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I've an HTTP custom service created in a SAP Public Cloud environment.

Inside the service I'm using standard BO I_SALESORDERTP to create a Sales Order with partners/texts/items using the information I get in the json request of the HTTP service.

The next step I need to do is to register a payment over the same sales order I created before, but I'm not able to find the option inside I_SALESORDERTP. There is no entity to do this and no fields in the Heder to entity to enter payment information.

Is there a way to create a sales order payment via ABAP code? I saw that there is an standard API (CE_SALESORDER) that has some entities related to payments but I'm not sure if I can call it from ABAP code inside the service.

Thanks and regards!

entry.title: ABAP RAP Custom Entity with Parameter not allowed
entry.content:
Hi Gurus,

@Ramjeekorada @Ramjee_korada 

I have created custom entity and Implemented if_rap_query_provider~select method. Service I created in V4 API.

This custom entity is with NO parameter is working as expected and returning all results.

When I added parameter to this Custom Entity It started giving me below Error ->

Entity 'ZCE_TU_DETAILS': Parameter entity set '$PARAMETER$ZCE_TU_DETAILS' cannot be used for read

I do not know how to test or use this Custom Entity with parameter... please help pointing to any documentation or example...

I have followed below documentation:

https://help.sap.com/docs/abap-cloud/abap-rap/using-parameters-in-unmanaged-query?version=sap_btp 

Please help...

 

Thanks-

entry.title: notes 3144435 not support S4CORE 108
entry.content:
--- Product/Function ---

No predicted product

User selected product: SAP S/4HANA (Recommended & Recent)

No predicted product function

User selected product function: SAP S/4HANA > Master Data > Material/Product Data Management (Recent)

Predicted component: LO-MD-MM (Product Function)

 

--- TagID/AreaID ---

TagID:

AreaID:

entry.title: Re: call SE11 Search Help through ABAP code.
entry.content:
Hi,
I'm new to abap and i have the same scenario. Have you resolved your concern? if so, can I ask for a solution. I want to get the return values from my elementary search help created in se11. Other FM's only allow to show the search help itself but I want to get multiple return values. Please help

entry.title: Re: What is the file storage limit per user for Sales and Service Cloud V2? If the limit is exceeded
entry.content: Refer to the product supplement. SAP Sales Cloud and Service Cloud Version 2 up to 10 GB per user and 10 TB for the entire system. https://www.sap.com/australia/about/trust-center/agreements/cloud/cloud-services.html?sort=latest_desc&search=sales%20and%20service&tag=language%3Aenglish&pdf-asset=c8001d74-a97e-0010-bca6-c68f7e60039b&page=1

entry.title: Sales BOM
entry.content:
Hello, 

A requirement to maintain Sales Bill of Materials (BOM) with specific criteria : 

The header level (A) should contain a text item with a value, while the component level (B, C) should consist of stock items that can be issued without value.

We already tried ERLA and LUMF at the header level to address this issue. But doesn't work.. 

 In the past, we used "Assign Item Categories" and VL07 (Create / Maintain Item Categories) to modify item category assignments. However, in the Public cloud, the system does not allow us to create or change item category assignments.

Thank you,
 SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Sales

entry.title: Re: SAP IAG Integration with SAP Ariba - MultiERP Architecture (FPC Realms)
entry.content:
Hi Soum,

Much Appreciated for the blog. Can you please share your experience on other features of IAG such as Access Analysis  , Role designer and Access certification with Ariba

Regards

Plaban

entry.title: Re: Upload Excel using SAP RAP Only
entry.content: I found that there is an issue with uploading CSV files. Can you please confirm again

entry.title: How can we have one inspection lot generated for multiple batches during GR for a PO?
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts. I need your support and feedbacks to get to a solution to meet my clients requirement. 

As per standard SAP, for every batch created during Goods Receipt there is an Inspection lot created but our requirement is for multiple batches created during GR, we want only one Inspection lot to be created.

Is it possible to meet this requirement in standard SAP only with configurations or settings or an enhancement is a must?

Kindly share your thoughts and expert advise.

Note: We do not have EWM and have ruled out any settings related to this. Batch is created manually and have external batch numbering

Regards,

Lenin

entry.title: Set Up Integration Suite Error APIMGT100: Activation of API Management capability has failed.
entry.content:
When I activate the capabilities in Integration Suite, I encounter the following error: "Error APIMGT100: Activation of API Management capability has failed. Please try again later."

Does anyone have a solution for this issue?
Thank you in advance!

SAP Integration Suite SAP Integration Strategy

entry.title: Re: Handling Unit (HU) Label Printing Through PPF in SAP EWM
entry.content: it's really great sharing and big contribution !! Thumbsup👍

entry.title: Cloud ERP two-tier Confluence Event Highlights – Blog collection
entry.content:
We are excited to share highlights of Cloud ERP Two-tier Confluence Event through this blog series.

Reflecting on the event, we delved into an overview, master data integration, various sales office scenarios and recent innovations. This blog series aims to help users better understand Two-tier ERP with  SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition  

Two-tier ERP addresses groups of companies including headquarters and subsidiaries. It comprises a headquarters ERP with high landscape complexity and being highly customized, and a subsidiaries ERP to support quick scale at lower costs with low landscape complexity. Both headquarters and subsidiaries ERP are integrated in a hybrid landscape with suitable implementation and process optimizations

Check below blog links to know updates from each of the areas

  • Two-tier ERP Overview
  • Two-tier ERP Master Data Replication Options
  • Two-tier ERP Transactional Data Replication options 
  • Sales Office scenarios in Two-tier ERP 
  • Two-tier ERP with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408

To know more click on " What is two-tier ERP? " page on sap.com

You can also reach out two-tier ERP community for more clarifications

Stay tuned for in-depth posts on each topic

entry.title: Re: Highlights of Cloud ERP two-tier Confluence Event – Blog collection
entry.content: This blog post was inadvertently published as a question instead of an article. Please check new article

entry.title: SYST: Period 000 is not valid in financial year variant K4 Message no. FGV003 in CK11N
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

While run Material Cost Estimate with Qty Structure in CK11N where user getting below error to specific one material for other material they can run CK11N successfully. 

we are using FY K4, could anyone please tell me where the problem fix 

SYST: Period 000 is not valid in financial year variant K4

Message no. FGV003

entry.title: Re: SAP Companion content - Moving from test to production
entry.content:
First impression: you did a lot of potentially unnecessary actions: I mean moving SAP Enable Now content from one workarea to another, instead of just applying the same SAP Companion configuration to you Prod system.

If nobody (even authors in the editor mode) can see the content you migrated, the Chrome or Edge browser developer tools are your best friends to spot the issue. If there is something wrong, with the authentication or else, you will see it in the developer tool console.

entry.title: Re: Highlights of Cloud ERP two-tier Confluence Event – Blog collection
entry.content: This blog post was inadvertently published as a question instead of an article. you can check new and updated blog link > https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/cloud-erp-two-tier-confluence-event-highlights-blog-collection/ba-p/13894573#M58109

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi @David_Chaviano ,

I am currently an educational consultant working in SAP Logistics module. I think this opportunity would be an amazing way to start me on a new career as an SAP solution consultant. I loved your analogy about the amusement park experience and I certainly identify as one of those people who would love to learn more about the bigger picture about how businesses operate, improve their processes and provide quality products and/or services to their customers. 

I believe this digital skills initiative is a great way to pay it forward and help those take the first step in transforming their lives. Embracing this new challenge aligns perfectly with my passion for continuous learning and desire to contribute to meaningful advancements in the maritime supply chain ecosystem.

Thank you for this wonderful opportunity, 

Hana.

entry.title: Re: Highlights of Cloud ERP two-tier Confluence Event – B...
entry.content: Hi Esther, you were right . This blog post was inadvertently published as a question instead of an article. you can check new and updated blog link > https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/cloud-erp-two-tier-confluence... Regards,Shiva

entry.title: Re: Upgrade SAP enabale Now 2406 start ManagerSEN failled
entry.content: Another hint: don't 100% trust the SAP Enable Now Manager Installation Guide when it advises you to modify the config.properties file prior to deploying the Manager .war file. Just skip this entire "Pre-Parametrization of the Installer" chapter. 99.9% of customers I've dealt with broke the .war file when following this advice. Just use the original Manager .war file and everything will run smoothly, provided other software and hardware requirements are met.

entry.title: Re: call SE11 Search Help through ABAP code.
entry.content:
Didn't you find this answer during your search?

(If the answers aren't clear enough for you or are no longer valid, better ask your own question in Q&A)

entry.title: Re: Welcome to our SAP Learning Group moderated by SAP Learning Experts
entry.content: Hello Everyone, My name is Chauncey Clay, and I’m excited to be a part of this SAP Learning Community!I’m currently working towards my SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with the Vower learning group, and I’m really looking forward to expanding my knowledge.

entry.title: No entry in table T589A for P
entry.content:
Hi All ,

Getting below error while hiring an employee through PA40

No entry in table T589A for P.

I have created dynamic action as below but unable to find an error.

0000 04 11 P T001P-MOLGA='10'
0000 04 12 P P0000-MASSN='UH'
0000 04 13 I INS,0041,,,(P0000-BEGDA),(P0000-ENDDA)/D
0000 04 14 W P0041-DAR01='01'
0000 04 15 W P0041-DAT01=P0000-BEGDA
0000 04 16 W P0041-DAR02='V1'
0000 04 17 W P0041-DAT02=P0000-BEGDA

and also checked T589A table and all entries are there with P

P COP COP 03 12
P CRP INS 03 12
P DEL DEL 04 00
P DIS DIS 01 00
P DIS2 DIS 01 00
P EDQ EDQ 05 00
P FAST FAST 02 00
P FDEL FDEL 04 00
P FDIS FDIS 01 00
P FEDQ FEDQ 05 00
P FINS FINS 03 00
P FMOD FMOD 02 00
P INS INS 03 12
P INSS INS 03 12
P LIS0 LIS0 01 11
P LIS1 LIS1 02 11
P LIS2 LIS2 02 11

P LIS9 LIS9 04 10
P MOD MOD 02 00
P SEQ MOD 02 00

 

Please help on same.

Regards,

Joe.

entry.title: Block Planning tab has no details for characteristics in PDS view screen | S4 Hana | CURTO_SIMU |
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

I have a query regarding Block Planning with PDS. I have setup a block planning scenario in my system and the blocks are correctly picking the relevant characteristics orders as per the block basis definition maintained, however, when I am checking the PDS with /n/SAPAPO/CURTO_SIMU transaction, I can see that even though the 'Block Planning Class' assignment is shown correctly, the 'Block Planning' tab for the activity is empty and having no values for characteristics. I want to know am I missing any config. or any master data setup is missing here? Please see the attached screenshot for reference.

Appreciate your response in advance.

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud for planning - Advanced Formula "...
entry.content:
Hi @guenay

Actually, there is no limitation on the number of Dimensions for the Linked(Source) Model in the LINK() function. 

Please check first whether the Dimensions of the Default & Linked model are Public or Local. If there are local dimensions in the Linked model, they should be mapped in Link() statements.

Second, the fact data of the Linked model should be in Hierarchy member of Dimensions. If there are "Not in hierarchy members" in the Source data & dimension member, it is excluded from the calculation scope. So, any data is not copied to the Default model. Please check it and if there are fact data on the Not in hierarchy member, add the configuration "HIERARCHY.INCLUDE_MEMBERS_NOT_IN_HIERARCHY" in your data action script. 

  • MODEL [PlanModel]
        HIERARCHY.INCLUDE_MEMBERS_NOT_IN_HIERARCHY = [d/Dimension_1], [d/Dimension_2]
        MEMBERSET [d/Dimension_x] = ....
    ENDMODEL

 

Moonjun

entry.title: Re: Enable Now - PowerBI Data Point for User Roles?
entry.content:
Hey, @andrewpickstone, 

This is an easy task, provided you know how to work with the Edge/Chrome developer tools. With them one can easily reveal all the API calls the SAP Enable Now Manager makes to get the list of users or their roles.

Get the list of users: <my-sen-system>.enable-now.cloud.sap/!/user

Get the list of roles in SAP Enable Now: <my-sen-system>.enable-now.cloud.sap/!/group/*?%7B%22search%22:%5B%7B%22param%22:%22active%22,%22operation%22:%22=%22,%22value%22:%221%22%7D%5D%7D%20

Get a list of users assigned to a role: <my-sen-system>.enable-now.cloud.sap/!/group/<ROLE_UUID>/identity?%7B%22order%22:%5B%7B%22param%22:%22type%22,%22direction%22:%22asc%22%7D%5D%7D

entry.title: Re: How to integrate SAP Project and Resource Management...
entry.content:
Hi,

There are multiple options to connect non-SAP 3rd Party HXM solution with the SAP Projects and Resource management. they are

  1. SAP Integration suite.
  2. Connecting 3rd Party Non-SAP Solution with S4HC

Case 1:

"Non-SAP 3rd party HXM Solution -> SAP Integration Suite -> MDI/MDO -> SAP Projects and Resource Management"

You can refer to the blog that clearly describes the 3rd party integrations with help of Integration suite + MDI/MDO "https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-integration-suite-integration-with-sap-master-data-integration-mdi/ba-p/13525349" .  

Case 2:

Via the API's you can connect the 3rd Party Non-SAP HXM Solution with the S4HC system. Once you have the WF Persons in S4HC you can setup the WF Person replication from S4HC -> MDI/MDO -> SAP P&RM. the step by step process is explained below.

Pre-requisite is 6JO scope is already in scope and activated. 

  1. Bring basic worker information ( workforce person & employment information) to S/4HANA Cloud from 3rd party system. S/4HC already has integration of worker data to RM. This is via pre-built integration between S/4HC and RM and here also we use MDI. But please remember that integration is already provided by SAP.
  2. Bring Workforce Availability information to RM via APIs provided by SAP P&RM - 

    https://api.sap.com/api/WorkforceAvailability/schema

  3. Bring Workforce Skills to RM via API provided by SAP P&RM - https://api.sap.com/api/ProjectExperience/schema

Regards,

entry.title: Issue with Zero Net Value in Sales Order After Updating Billing Plan Using BAPIs
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are currently facing an issue with our sales order creation process. Here's the sequence of BAPIs we are using:

1.DIPCOL_COLLECTIVE_RRB
2.BAPI_SALESORDER_CHANGE
For billing plan updates, we are using:
3.SD_VBKD_READ_WITH_VBELN
4.BILLING_SCHEDULE_READ
5.BILLING_SCHEDULE_SAVE


With this logic, we are able to create and update sales orders, and the billing plan updates perfectly. However, the sales orders are being created with a zero net value. If we exclude the billing plan-related BAPIs (SD_VBKD_READ_WITH_VBELN, BILLING_SCHEDULE_READ, BILLING_SCHEDULE_SAVE), the net value of the sales order calculates correctly.

We have already raised an OSS message, and SAP replied that the FM SD_SALES_BILLINGPLAN_CHANGE is not released. They suggested discussing the issue with an other SAP consultant or making use of the SAP Community.

Has anyone encountered a similar issue or have suggestions on how to resolve this?

Any help or guidance would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Case Sensitivity Issue with ListPicker in SAP MDK 24.4
entry.content:
Hi community,

I recently developed an application in SAP MDK where I'm using a ListPicker control connected to an OData service. Previously, this control was not case-sensitive, so it didn't matter whether the search text had uppercase or lowercase letters.However, since updating to version 24.4 of MDK, I've noticed a change in behavior. Now, when performing a search in the ListPicker, the entered text must exactly match the format stored in the database. For example, if the value is stored as "HOUSE" in the database and I type "House," no matches are found.

Has anyone else experienced this case sensitivity issue with the ListPicker? I'd appreciate any insights or suggestions on how to ignore case sensitivity or if there’s a workaround for this behavior.

Thanks in advance for any help you can provide!

Do you have any knowledge about this behavior? @Jitendra_Canal

Kind regards!!

Huan.

entry.title: Building an Effective Testing Architecture for SAP Implementations
entry.content:
Implementing SAP S/4HANA is a complex, multi-phased project that requires a robust and systematic testing architecture to ensure success. A well-structured testing framework not only safeguards the quality and functionality of the system but also mitigates risks associated with data integrity, performance, and overall system behavior. This article outlines a comprehensive approach to building an effective testing architecture for SAP implementations, breaking it down into four key phases: Prepare, Explore, Realize, and Deploy. 

Prepare: Laying the Foundation 

The initial phase in any SAP S/4HANA implementation involves thorough planning and strategy development. This phase guarantees that all testing activities are in sync with the overall business objectives and technical requirements. 

  • Test Plan & Strategy: Before any testing activities can commence, a detailed test plan is critical. This involves outlining the scope, objectives, types of testing to be executed, and timelines. The strategy also accounts for resource allocation, risk management, and defining test environments. 

A well-defined strategy at this stage provides the foundation for all subsequent testing activities, ensuring clarity and direction. This proactive approach facilitates a seamless transition into the next phases and minimizes the chances of overlooking crucial components.

Explore: Defining Scenarios and Test Cases 

In the Explore phase, the business processes and functionalities concerning SAP standards are analyzed, allowing the team to align testing with key project deliverables. 

  • Fit-to-Standard Workshops: These workshops are conducted to assess the business processes and determine whether they fit into SAP’s standard functionalities. Any gaps identified will help shape custom development or configuration requirements. 
  • Solution Design Document (SDD): The SDD defines the functional and technical design of the system based on the results of the workshops. It serves as the foundation for creating test scenarios. 
  • Test Scenarios & Cases: Based on the design, specific test scenarios are developed. These scenarios simulate real-world usage of the system, and detailed test cases are generated. Test cases include expected outcomes, validation points, and acceptance criteria. 

This phase is essential for ensuring that all test cases are tied directly to the functional and business requirements, making sure that no critical feature is left untested. By establishing a clear linkage between business processes and testing, organizations can enhance the effectiveness of their testing efforts.

Realize: Core Testing Activities 

The Realize phase involves the bulk of testing efforts. This phase ensures that the SAP S/4HANA system is built, configured, and tested according to the documented requirements. 

  • Development: The development team begins configuring the system, and once initial configurations are ready, they are handed over for testing. This collaborative approach ensures that developers remain engaged in the testing process. 
  • Quality Assurance & Smoke Testing: Smoke testing is performed to ensure that the major functionalities are stable and ready for further testing. It serves as a checkpoint, permitting only functional and stable builds to advance to more in-depth testing stages. 
  • Functional Testing: Functional testing ensures that individual SAP modules perform according to the requirements outlined in the SDD. It is typically conducted by QA teams familiar with the system’s functionalities. 

System Integration Testing (SIT) 

SIT is a critical step, as it tests the integration of different modules and components within SAP and between SAP and other systems. This testing ensures seamless data flow and interaction across different subsystems. 

  • Performance & Security Testing: In parallel with functional testing, performance tests are conducted to ensure the system can handle the required load and user activity. Security testing evaluates vulnerabilities in the system, ensuring compliance with security protocols and data protection laws. 
  • Training: Throughout the testing process, key users are trained to understand the system and its functionalities, which ensures proper user adoption post-deployment. 
  • Go/No-Go Decision: Once all tests are completed, a Go/No-Go decision is made. If all test cases pass, the system is ready for User Acceptance Testing (UAT); if not, necessary adjustments are made. This decision-making process is pivotal in maintaining the integrity of the implementation timeline. 

Deploy: Final Validation and Cutover 

The Deploy phase is the concluding step where the system is tested and validated in a real-world environment prior to its official launch. 

  • User Acceptance Testing (UAT): UAT is crucial as it allows real users to assess the system in practical environments, ensuring it fulfils business objectives. UAT support teams provide help to users throughout this phase, ensuring smooth execution. 
  • Mock Cutover: A mock cutover is performed before the actual cutover to validate the cutover process. This includes testing data migration and ensuring all system functionalities work as expected. 
  • Final Smoke Test & Sign-off: A final smoke test is conducted post-mock cutover to ensure the system is stable and ready for production. Once the test passes, the stakeholders give their final sign-off, leading to the official cutover. 
  • Production Cutover: After successful mock testing and final smoke testing, the production cutover is executed. Data validation and testing continue during this phase to ensure no discrepancies occur during the transition to the live environment. 

Leveraging Tools for SAP Implementation Testing 

Organizations can enhance their testing processes through various specialized tools: 

  1. Onapsis: Secures SAP systems by identifying security vulnerabilities and ensuring compliance with standards like GDPR and SOX. It performs real-time security assessments, crucial during the Realize and Deploy phases, to minimize risks before go-live. 
  2. Tricentis: Automates functional and regression testing, speeding up test execution and improving coverage during the Realize phase. AI-powered risk-based testing prioritizes critical test cases, optimizing testing efforts and ensuring seamless updates. 
  3. LoadRunner: Simulates various user loads to assess SAP system performance under stress. It identifies bottlenecks and ensures scalability, helping teams fine-tune the system during the Realize phase for smooth high-volume transactions. 
  4. NexAI: Leverages AI to enhance test case optimization and anticipate potential failures. It improves testing efficiency in the Explore and Realize phases, enabling proactive issue resolution and ensuring a more resilient SAP implementation. 
  5. Micro Focus: Centralizes test management, defect tracking, and reporting. It ensures thorough execution of test cases, tracks issues, and facilitates collaboration, enhancing overall quality control throughout the SAP lifecycle. 
  6. UiPath: Automates routine tasks such as executing tests, validating data, and performing regression testing. This boosts efficiency during the Realize and Deploy phases, reducing manual intervention and speeding up the testing process. 

Conclusion 

Implementing a structured and well-documented testing architecture is essential for ensuring the success of an SAP S/4HANA implementation. Each phase of testing—Prepare, Explore, Realize, and Deploy—has a distinct set of activities that build on each other to validate the system’s functionality, performance, and security. By leveraging tools like Tricentis, LoadRunner, and UiPath, organizations can automate and streamline their testing processes, ensuring timely and effective delivery of their SAP projects.

entry.title: How to set future start date and end date based on current year in MDF object
entry.content:
Hi Experts

Need your expertise on  How to set start date as "Next Year Start Date" and end date as "Next Year End date" by validating new born child's date of birth in MDF object.

example:- 

If child's date of birth is 21/Sep/2024

then need to set custom date as follows,

Start date Account 1: 01/Jan/2025

End date Account 1: 31/Dec/2025

Start date Account 2: 01/Jan/2026

End date Account 2: 31/Dec/2026

The set up has to behave dynamically to read the dob of new child and set future date.

Thanks

Arun

entry.title: Re: Transportation Management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 (FPS02) - What's new?
entry.content:
Hi Eric,

Thanks for sharing the details.

Could you please clarify if the integration of JIS and JIT calls with SAP TM would work for OEM to Suppliers? To be more precise receiving of goods from the Suppliers/Just in time supply to production (Inbound Logistics for OEM).

Kindly advise.

Thanks and Regards,
Saravana Kumar Subbiah

entry.title: Re: Restrict logon through RFC remote logon
entry.content:
Hello Panos,

this is known as RFC hopping. To prevent this, don’t store credentials in rfc destinations from systems of lower security classification to system of higher (e.g., dev->prod, qas->prod). Make sure the emergency user can only be used in emergency cases by locking it when not in use and rotate the password frequently. Enable the security audit log for this user and implement a process for evidence which person has used the emergency user at which point in time.


The secinfo is not considered for such connections.

Br,

Joe

entry.title: Re: Setting Up the Crossboarding Process with SAP SuccessFactors Recruitment Management
entry.content:
Thanks @SachinPachauri for the step by step procedure for setting up crossboarding. 

I have a small doubt and don't get the exact rationale that why Job information record is updated directly as soon the onboarding is initiated from recruitment. Ideally system must update the Job information record only after completion of RNHDR step. 

Also, do we need to configure any field in Job info to clearly distinguish the Transfer records that are coming from Recruitment vs direct entering in Job Info. We hadn't created any field "Source system for Onboarding" and untill then under Process object, it kept showing "Internal Hire initiated from Employee central".

Is there an easy way to distinguish the record updates that are happening via Crossboarding vs direct update in EC?

entry.title: Re: How to avoid in a story that tables are refreshed wit...
entry.content: If it's an acquired model, you may try this option from model configuration.

entry.title: Re: Design iFlow to accept multiple PDF Files using the H...
entry.content:
see design guidelines covering atatchment handling at https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/sap-integration-suite/attachment-handling

for the sample flows we even use http sender adapter

Alex

entry.title: Re: An Introduction to Enterprise Event Enablement for SAP BTP ABAP Environment
entry.content:
Hi Safa,

I'm trying to test the events by publishing on Event mesh. The config in S4 is in place. However, I don't see events reaching S4 HANA. The messages are released from the event mesh queue. What would be the best way to troubleshoot this?

Also,I saw another blog which mentioned to define Communication scenario. I guess to consume events in S4 HANA from event mesh queue , it is not needed?

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA Cloud: Field Extension Wizard in S/4HANA Cloud 2408
entry.content:
Dear @taranam and @Andre_Fischer ,
Happy to see that SAP is developing such nice features which eases the developer's work and they could concentrate more on the business extension and logics. 

But what keeps me puzzled is that why there is no extension possible when it comes to many of the standard Business Object Behaviors. When we wanted to extend some of the standard behavior like you mentioned in the session DT280 | Build clean core extensions with ABAP Cloud for SAP S/4HANA Cloud which could be adding a validation or determination, this is now not possible when the standard behavior misses the keyword extensible. When does SAP provide a behavior as extensible?

Regards
Arunkumar AS

entry.title: Re: SAP Integration Suite and IOT - protocol MQTT
entry.content:
there is a partner adapter offering for MQTT, see https://hub.sap.com/package/KaTeMQTTAdapter/overview

Alex
